_doc: Models that legitimately fail at create-time (model build / config) due to known unfixed bugs. The sweep skips these entirely AND validates that no NEW create_error appears outside this list. New create_errors during a sweep MUST be either (a) added here with a reason, or (b) fixed at root. Goal: zero unexplained create_errors per sweep, so setup-side issues never mask real graph-break improvements/regressions.
_workflow: 1. To add an entry: append to `entries[]` with model, modes, error_pattern (substring of the actual error message), added (ISO date), reason (one-line + issue link if applicable).
2. The sweep skips any (model, mode) pair listed here. Skip is logged (`Skipped N known-create-error work items`).

3. After identify pass, the sweep validates: every create_error in results must match a known entry's (model, mode, error_pattern). Unmatched ones print a loud warning and (with --strict-create-errors) cause a non-zero exit.

4. To remove an entry: when the underlying bug is fixed (or worked around), delete the line. Next sweep will re-test the model and surface the new status (full_graph / graph_break / or a NEW create_error worth investigating).

entries.reason: tie_weights_keys definition bug surfaced by Animesh fullgraph sweep. Real corpus or HF-side bug; not an env issue.

transformers config drift — Qwen3OmniMoeTalkerTextConfig missing 'shared_expert_intermediate_size' attribute. Real bug — likely a transformers version mismatch with the model's expected config. Surfaced by Animesh fullgraph sweep.
